---
title: "trigger_days_loop"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
#| message: false

library(tidyverse)
library(reticulate)

# Use venv Python ~/.venv/pytrigger
use_python("~/.venv/pytrigger/bin/python", required = TRUE)
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false

import sys
sys.path.append("..")  # make 'python/' package visible

import time
import datetime as dt
import pandas as pd
from trigger import TriggerDB
from tqdm.auto import tqdm

from python.trigger_utils import get_active_emails, silent_logger

# =======================================================
# 0. CONFIGURATION (these could become function arguments)
# =======================================================

# Subjects (here: active account emails)
EMAILS = get_active_emails(limit=200)[0:2]

# Date range (inclusive)
START_DATE = dt.date(2025, 10, 1)
END_DATE   = dt.date(2025, 10, 5)

# Table name
TABLE_NAME = "myair"

# Columns to select
SELECT_COLUMNS = [
    "email",
    "year", "month", "day",
    "hour", "minute", "second",
    "pm1",
]

# Maximum number of rows per query
MAX_ROWS_PER_QUERY = 5000

# Delay between iterations (in milliseconds)
DELAY_MS = 50  # To prevent server crash??

# =======================================================
# 1. DERIVED OBJECTS FROM CONFIGURATION
# =======================================================

# Sequence of days
days = pd.date_range(start=START_DATE, end=END_DATE, freq="D")

# Total iterations for the progress bar
total_iterations = len(EMAILS) * len(days)

# Container for partial DataFrames
all_dfs = []

# Counters for progress info
current_rows = 0
current_bytes = 0

# =======================================================
# 2. MAIN EXTRACTION LOOP
# =======================================================

with TriggerDB() as db, tqdm(total=total_iterations,
                             desc="Extracting data",
                             ncols=100) as pbar:

    for email in EMAILS:
        for day in days:
            year_val = day.year
            month_val = day.month
            day_val = day.day

            # Build filters specific to (email, day)
            loop_filters = {
              "hour": ">=0",       # FIXED
              "year":  f"={year_val}",
              "month": f"={month_val}",
              "day":   f"={day_val}",
              "email": f"={email}"
            }

            # Build and run the query with logs silenced
            with silent_logger():
                query = (
                    db.from_(TABLE_NAME)
                      .select(*SELECT_COLUMNS)
                      .where(**loop_filters)
                      .limit(MAX_ROWS_PER_QUERY)
                )
                rows = query.fetch()

            if rows:
                # Normalize JSON rows into a flat DataFrame
                df = pd.json_normalize(rows)
                all_dfs.append(df)

                # Update counters
                current_rows += len(df)
                current_bytes += df.memory_usage(deep=True).sum()

            # Convert bytes → MB
            size_mb = current_bytes / (1024 * 1024)

            # Update progress bar postfix and step
            pbar.set_postfix({
                "rows": current_rows,
                "MB": f"{size_mb:.2f}",
            })
            pbar.update(1)

            # Optional delay to avoid overloading the server
            if DELAY_MS > 0:
                time.sleep(DELAY_MS)

# =======================================================
# 3. FINAL CONCATENATION
# =======================================================

if all_dfs:
    data_all = pd.concat(all_dfs, ignore_index=True)
else:
    data_all = pd.DataFrame()

```
